findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the SSM slot `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` already exists in ssm.tf (line 49) and terraform apply is a no-op for it; the operative step is `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --value <PAT>`.
evidence: infra/terraform/ssm.tf line 49: "/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat" already present in rebar_secret_params toset
ssm.tf line 60-73: aws_ssm_parameter.rebar_secrets for_each creates all slots including reviewbot-tickets-pat with lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }
Plan says 'populate the PAT' as a separate step, implying awareness — but the 'Apply terraform' framing still incorrectly implies a new resource creation
findings.finding: The plan says 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' implying the SSM parameter slot needs to be created by terraform, but /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat is already declared in ssm.tf (line 49) and exists in the rebar_secret_params locals block. The operative step is only populating its value out-of-band (aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite), not a terraform resource creation. The 'terraform apply' framing may cause confusion about what the apply actually does.
findings.location: What section: 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)'
scenarios: Executor runs terraform plan expecting to see a new resource and sees no-op, becomes uncertain whether the plan is correct or the slot is missing
Executor skips the terraform apply step thinking there is nothing to do, and also skips the intended value-population step
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' with 'Populate the existing SSM slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat (already declared in ssm.tf) via aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite; terraform apply is not needed for this slot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the plan's 'Apply terraform' step: terraform apply only asserts the SSM slot exists (it already does, so this is a no-op for the value); the operative provisioning step is an explicit `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <PAT>` that must be called out separately.
evidence: ssm.tf line 49: '/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat' already present in rebar_secret_params
ssm.tf lines 67-69: lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] } — terraform will not overwrite an operator-set value, and will not SET the real PAT from a fresh apply
fetch-secrets.sh line 91: get_param_optional used — a CHANGEME/empty value yields blank without aborting, making the failure silent
Plan conflates 'apply terraform' (creates/asserts the slot exists) with 'populate the PAT' (set the real secret value) without distinguishing the two operations
findings.finding: The plan says 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' as if the slot must be created, but ssm.tf already contains that parameter in rebar_secret_params (line 49) with lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] } — terraform apply is a no-op for this slot's value. The operative step is 'aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite ... --value <PAT>'; if the implementer assumes terraform apply populates the PAT, the REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT will remain empty/CHANGEME and fetch-secrets will silently yield a blank value (get_param_optional never aborts).
findings.location: What section — 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat), populate the PAT'
scenarios: Implementer runs terraform apply believing it provisions the PAT, skips the explicit aws ssm put-parameter step; fetch-secrets writes REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT= (blank); bot starts without the PAT; artifact emission silently skips; AC1 fails with no error.
findings.suggested_fix: Split into two named steps: (1) 'Confirm SSM slot exists (terraform apply is a no-op here — slot already in ssm.tf)'; (2) 'Populate the PAT: aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <real-PAT>'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a trigger for env-only changes in the autodeploy fix: either (a) run fetch-secrets unconditionally on every deploy tick (outside any `changed` gate, before the BOT_PATHS block, with a `docker compose up -d` reload if .env actually changed), or (b) add a dedicated SECRETS_PATHS trigger (e.g. keyed to ssm.tf or a sentinel file) that re-runs fetch-secrets and restarts the bot even when BOT_PATHS is unchanged.
evidence: autodeploy.sh lines 180-217: the only docker compose up -d call is inside `if changed "$BOT_PATHS"` — fetch-secrets injected here only fires on source changes
BOT_PATHS = 'src/rebar/ infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot pyproject.toml infra/compose/docker-compose.yml' (line 55) — no SSM or .env path included
The plan's own problem statement: 'NO future env key can reach the box without a boot' — the fix must handle env-only changes, not only code+env changes

ABSENCE: the plan does not specify a trigger mechanism for env-only deploys (e.g. a separate fetch-secrets path outside BOT_PATHS, a dedicated SECRETS_PATHS trigger, or unconditional fetch-secrets on every deploy tick)
findings.finding: The proposed env-delivery fix ('autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up') is mechanically broken for the exact scenario the plan names: a new SSM param added with NO source-code change. In autodeploy.sh, fetch-secrets would only be invoked inside the `if changed "$BOT_PATHS"` block (the only place `docker compose up -d` is called). A new env key (e.g. a future REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT being newly populated, or any future SSM-backed key) with no corresponding change to BOT_PATHS would never trigger that block, leaving the .env stale exactly as before — violating AC2 ('New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot').
findings.location: What section — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'; AC: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot'
scenarios: A future operator adds a new SSM param (e.g. a webhook signing key) and sets its value; no source file in BOT_PATHS changes; autodeploy runs, sees no BOT_PATHS diff, skips the block, never runs fetch-secrets; the new .env key never reaches the container until a manual boot.

The current plan's own PAT activation: if the PAT is populated AFTER the last code deploy, re-running fetch-secrets manually once is required — but the plan's AC2 promises this won't be needed in future.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact placement and trigger condition for the fetch-secrets.sh re-run in autodeploy.sh: state whether it fires on every deploy tick, only on BOT_PATHS changes, or unconditionally before `docker compose up -d`, and specify the error-handling contract (abort deploy? warn-and-continue?) to preserve autodeploy's fail-safe guarantee.
evidence: autodeploy.sh line 88: RSYNC_EXCLUDES includes '--exclude infra/compose/.env' — .env is explicitly never synced.
No fetch-secrets.sh invocation exists anywhere in autodeploy.sh (confirmed by grep).
Plan uses 'e.g.' — indicating the fix placement is not yet decided, making this an unspecified processing protocol.

autodeploy.sh has three distinct trigger paths (BOT_PATHS, OBS_PATHS, CERTBOT_PATHS) each with different materialization semantics; the plan does not specify which trigger condition, if any, should gate the fetch-secrets re-run.
findings.finding: The 'design fix for env delivery on deploy' is specified only as 'e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' — the placement within autodeploy.sh's existing flow is ambiguous (before rsync? after rsync? only on a BOT_PATHS change? always?). autodeploy.sh explicitly excludes .env from rsync (line 88) and has no fetch-secrets call anywhere in its flow; adding one without specifying WHERE in the decision tree it fires (and under what condition) leaves the implementation underspecified as a protocol co-location gap.
findings.location: What section: 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'; File: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh
scenarios: Implementer adds fetch-secrets.sh invocation before the BOT_PATHS block — fetch-secrets fails (SSM unreachable), causing autodeploy to abort before building; this is a regression for the 'fail-safe, never half-updated' constraint autodeploy.sh explicitly requires.
Implementer adds it unconditionally at the top, turning every 2-minute poll into an SSM call even when there is no deploy — a performance/quota regression.
Implementer adds it only inside the BOT_PATHS changed block — new env keys for non-bot services still don't reach the box on deploy.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the autodeploy.sh change: specify failure handling for fetch-secrets.sh (e.g. log-and-continue with existing .env, or hard-fail with alerting), and document how to revert the change if deploys break.
evidence: 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
ABSENCE: no rollback path, no error-handling strategy, no fallback behavior (e.g. 'continue with existing .env on fetch failure') is described anywhere in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan adds a fetch-secrets.sh invocation inside autodeploy.sh (changing existing deploy behavior) with no stated rollback path or failure-handling strategy. If fetch-secrets.sh fails at deploy time (SSM unreachable, IAM permission error, missing parameter, etc.), all future automated deploys could be blocked or silently degraded, with no documented back-out plan.
findings.location: What section — 'autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up'; file infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh
scenarios: SSM endpoint is unreachable during a deploy; fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero; autodeploy.sh halts; service is not restarted after a code push — a deploy outage caused by the fix itself.
IAM role for the box loses SSM read permission; every subsequent autodeploy silently fails to restart the service.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for fetch-secrets.sh in autodeploy.sh: define whether failure should abort the deploy or fall back to the existing .env, add a timeout, and surface a clear error log so operators know the env was not refreshed.
evidence: fetch-secrets.sh calls AWS SSM — an external network call inside the deploy critical path
Plan specifies no retry, timeout, circuit-breaker, or 'proceed with existing .env' fallback
If SSM is down, autodeploy.sh either hard-fails (blocking all deploys) or silently proceeds with a stale/empty .env — neither outcome is addressed
findings.finding: Adding fetch-secrets.sh as a mandatory step inside autodeploy.sh introduces a hard SSM dependency into every deployment; no error handling, timeout, or fallback is specified for the case where SSM is unavailable or slow.
findings.location: ## What — 'autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up'; infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh
scenarios: AWS SSM has a transient outage during a hotfix deploy — the entire deploy is blocked
fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero and autodeploy.sh does not check the exit code, proceeding with a partially populated env and a bot that silently lacks the new keys
findings.suggested_fix: In autodeploy.sh: `if ! timeout 15 ./fetch-secrets.sh; then echo '[autodeploy] WARNING: fetch-secrets failed, proceeding with existing .env' >&2; fi` — or hard-abort with `set -e` if stale env is unacceptable. Document the chosen policy in a comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/fallback path for the autodeploy fetch-secrets integration: specify what happens if fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero (e.g. abort with alert vs. continue with stale .env) and document how to revert the autodeploy.sh change.
evidence: 'autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' — one-shot change to all deploy traffic
No mention of a flag, fallback, or revert procedure if fetch-secrets fails mid-deploy
AC: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot' — only happy path specified
findings.finding: The autodeploy fix (adding fetch-secrets.sh execution before compose up) is a behavior change affecting every future deploy, but no rollback path is specified — if fetch-secrets.sh hangs, errors, or has a credential gap, all subsequent deploys are broken with no stated recovery mechanism.
findings.location: ## What — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' / File: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh
scenarios: fetch-secrets.sh fails due to SSM IAM permission gap mid-deploy, blocking compose up and leaving the service down
fetch-secrets.sh network-times-out on every subsequent deploy after a credential rotation
findings.suggested_fix: In autodeploy.sh, run fetch-secrets.sh and gate on its exit code: if it fails, emit a clear error and either abort the deploy (fail-safe) or skip the secrets refresh and continue with the existing .env (fail-open), depending on desired policy. Document the revert as: remove/comment the fetch-secrets call to restore prior behavior.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-restart store-converge verification step to the activation sequence: specify the command or log check (e.g. `docker exec review-bot reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh` and confirm exit 0, or grep journald for ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR after the first review) that proves the tickets store is writable before closing AC1.
evidence: Plan says 'verify artifact emission works against the (now hand-created) gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume' with no verification command or protocol specified.

Dockerfile.reviewbot line 125-126: 'reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh || echo "reviewbot: tickets store ensure deferred"' — failure is silently swallowed; a missing .env-id store marker causes 100% of artifact emissions to fail with ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR.

reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh line 45-48: exits 0 without converging if the clone dir is not yet a git clone ('clone deferred') — so a restart after PAT population alone does not guarantee store convergence.

voter.py line 303-306: ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR is emitted on any emission failure, but the plan's AC1 ('no emission failed warnings') has no associated verification command to confirm the store is converged post-activation.
findings.finding: The plan's activation sequence ('populate PAT, re-run fetch-secrets.sh, restart bot') omits a required step: if the tickets clone was previously deferred (REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT was absent at the last entrypoint run), restarting the bot with the PAT now set will trigger a fresh clone attempt — but the plan does not specify whether the operator must explicitly confirm that reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh has converged the store (setting the .env-id marker) before declaring AC1 satisfied. The entrypoint silently swallows a ensure-tickets failure, so 'no emission failed warnings' can remain unverified without a specified check.
findings.location: What section: 'Apply terraform … re-run fetch-secrets.sh, restart bot; verify artifact emission works against the (now hand-created) gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume'
scenarios: Operator populates PAT, re-runs fetch-secrets.sh, restarts bot — clone succeeds but ensure-tickets.sh fails silently (e.g. rebar not importable, Python path issue). Operator declares AC1 satisfied without checking journald for ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR, and artifact emission remains broken.
First post-restart review emits ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR because .env-id is absent; operator has no specified verification step to catch this before closing the ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'After restart, confirm store convergence: run `docker exec <review-bot-container> reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh` and verify exit 0 + the final log line; then trigger a test review and confirm no ARTIFACT_EMIT_ERROR in journald.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether fetch-secrets.sh is re-run unconditionally on every autodeploy tick or conditionally (e.g. gated on a `changed()` trigger like OBS_PATHS/CERTBOT_PATHS), and justify the choice against the established conditional re-materialization pattern already in autodeploy.sh.
evidence: Plan text: 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up).'
autodeploy.sh lines 228-260: OBS_PATHS and CERTBOT_PATHS blocks gate re-materialization on `changed()` — the established prior art for this exact class of problem in this codebase.
autodeploy.sh line 88: RSYNC_EXCLUDES already protects .env; the gap is that fetch-secrets is never called on deploy, not that rsync breaks it.

fetch-secrets.sh lines 35-40: every run makes two IMDS calls + four-plus SSM get-parameter calls, all network-bound — unconditional runs on every 2-min tick adds meaningful latency and a new SSM availability dependency to the autodeploy hot path.
findings.finding: The plan proposes 'autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' as the env-delivery fix without evaluating the established path-triggered conditional re-materialization pattern already used for the same problem class in autodeploy.sh (OBS_PATHS/CERTBOT_PATHS blocks). Unconditional fetch-secrets re-runs would add 4+ SSM API calls and an IMDS round-trip to every 2-minute autodeploy tick — including no-op ticks where nothing changed — while the existing pattern gates re-materialization on changed() to avoid exactly this overhead.
findings.location: What section / 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
scenarios: Unconditional fetch-secrets in autodeploy means a transient SSM outage or IMDS hiccup can fail every autodeploy tick, blocking all deployments — the existing OBS/CERTBOT pattern avoids this by only touching SSM when the relevant source file actually changed.

A plan implementer reading 'e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' will likely wire it unconditionally (before the build step) rather than conditionally, diverging from the established codebase pattern without justification.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a SECRETS_PATHS trigger (e.g. 'infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh infra/terraform/ssm.tf') and call fetch-secrets conditionally via `if changed "$SECRETS_PATHS"` — matching the OBS_PATHS/CERTBOT_PATHS pattern already in the script. Note that this still doesn't cover the case of an operator populating a NEW SSM value without any code change; document that edge case and handle it (e.g. a manual fetch-secrets re-run + compose restart) as an operator step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism for delivering new env keys on deploy: state WHERE in autodeploy.sh fetch-secrets.sh is called (e.g. only when BOT_PATHS changed, or unconditionally before compose up), whether its failure is fatal or non-fatal to the deploy, and how the 'no SSM on transient' case is handled — 'e.g.' and 'design' are insufficient given this is the AC's core deliverable.
evidence: Plan text: 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
autodeploy.sh line 88: RSYNC_EXCLUDES includes '--exclude infra/compose/.env' — .env is deliberately excluded from every rsync; fetch-secrets.sh is never called
fetch-secrets.sh uses set -euo pipefail and exits 1 on any SSM failure, which would propagate to an autodeploy failure if called unconditionally
AC: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot' — but mechanism is unspecified
findings.finding: The autodeploy .env delivery fix is under-specified: the plan says 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' — the 'e.g.' and 'design' language leaves the concrete mechanism, invocation point, and failure handling unspecified. autodeploy.sh currently hard-excludes .env from rsync and never calls fetch-secrets.sh; a fetch-secrets.sh failure under set -euo pipefail would abort the entire deploy (including the bot rebuild), which is an unacceptable blast radius for an SSM transient.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy'; infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh RSYNC_EXCLUDES line 88
scenarios: SSM is transiently unavailable during an autodeploy tick: if fetch-secrets.sh is called unconditionally, the entire deploy aborts, blocking all bot rebuilds until SSM recovers.

fetch-secrets.sh is added to the wrong point in autodeploy.sh (e.g. before the 'changed' check), re-fetching on every tick even when nothing changed — unnecessary SSM calls and potential secret churn.
The fix is shipped to main but the exact invocation is left to the operator, creating the same 'pending-apply ledger' drift that ticket e983 was filed to address.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a targeted fetch-secrets.sh call in autodeploy.sh inside the `if changed "$BOT_PATHS"` block, BEFORE `docker compose up -d`, with a non-fatal error path (log + AUTODEPLOY_ERROR metric on SSM failure, but do not abort the deploy if the .env already exists — guard with `[ -f "$COMPOSE_DIR/.env" ]`). This preserves the fail-fast property on first boot while tolerating transient SSM unavailability on subsequent autodeploys.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the failure posture for fetch-secrets.sh when called from autodeploy: either (a) make it non-fatal/best-effort (only refresh optional vars, never exit 1 in the autodeploy path) or (b) document that SSM unavailability will trigger deploy backoff and add a distinct AUTODEPLOY_ERROR reason code so the alarm distinguishes it from a bot-build failure.
evidence: fetch-secrets.sh line 54: `if [ -z "${val}" ] || [ "${val}" = "None" ] || [ "${val}" = "CHANGEME" ]; then ... exit 1`
fetch-secrets.sh lines 81-84: required params (anthropic-api-key, mcp-hmac-signing-key, gerrit-admin-password, gerrit-bot-token) use fail-fast `get_param`, not `get_param_optional`

autodeploy.sh comment (line 18-19): 'replication.config / g2p / refs/meta/config changes are DETECT-ONLY (signalled for a manual operator apply): their correct apply needs a live-site copy + an SSM PAT re-fetch whose failure modes must not sit in the unattended path guarding a fail-closed gate'
Plan does not state whether the fetch-secrets call in autodeploy would be fatal or best-effort
findings.finding: The plan proposes injecting fetch-secrets.sh into the autodeploy path but does not specify the failure-mode interaction: fetch-secrets.sh is fail-fast (exit 1) on any required SSM param. If SSM is transiently unreachable during an autodeploy tick, this would block the deploy and trigger backoff — potentially freezing the fail-closed gate. The plan offers no precondition (e.g. non-fatal SSM fetch, or separate OPTIONAL-only re-fetch) to contain this new failure mode.
findings.location: What / 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
scenarios: SSM is transiently unreachable (endpoint hiccup, IAM propagation delay) during an autodeploy tick — fetch-secrets.sh exits 1, deploy backsoff, review-bot stays on stale image, gate stays in last-known-good state but no new code deploys until SSM recovers AND a new main SHA triggers a reset.
A required SSM param (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) has been accidentally cleared — every autodeploy tick fails fetch-secrets.sh and the gate is effectively frozen.
findings.suggested_fix: In autodeploy.sh, call a restricted variant of fetch-secrets that only refreshes OPTIONAL params (or wrap the call: `fetch-secrets.sh || { err fetch-secrets-failed "SSM unreachable during deploy; .env unchanged"; }` without `exit 1`) so a transient SSM outage degrades gracefully rather than blocking every deploy tick.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert AC3 from a file-path listing into a measurable outcome criterion, or remove it and note the relevant files inline in the What section where they are already referenced.
evidence: The line names files but states no observable change, behavioral outcome, or durable end-state
It cannot be evaluated as true or false — any edit to these files or none at all would 'satisfy' it
This is a scope-tagging annotation, not a criterion
findings.finding: AC3 ('File paths: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/terraform/ssm.tf') is a scope boundary listing, not a measurable acceptance criterion — it states no observable outcome and cannot be evaluated as 'pass/fail'.

scenarios: An agent ticks AC3 after touching the files without achieving the intended behavioral changes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live end-to-end acceptance criterion for AC2: specify that a test deploy is performed with a new canary SSM key, the key appears in the box's .env post-deploy without manual intervention, AND document the expected behavior when fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero (abort deploy, warn-and-continue, etc.).
evidence: AC2 states the outcome but names no verification method (test deploy, canary key, staging run, CI check)
No mention of fetch-secrets failure handling in the fix design or ACs
The new autodeploy path will be the DEFAULT for all future deploys once merged — proxy-validation risk: green AC without live path ever running
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new default behavior in autodeploy.sh (re-running fetch-secrets before compose up) but provides no testing plan or live end-to-end acceptance criterion for that path: AC2 ('New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot') can be checked off by code inspection or a dry-run without ever exercising the actual deploy pipeline with a real new key. Additionally, no failure-path coverage is specified — if fetch-secrets.sh exits non-zero mid-deploy (e.g. SSM unreachable, credentials expired), the plan does not state expected behavior or require it to be tested.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC2; ## What — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy'
scenarios: fetch-secrets.sh fails silently or with a non-zero exit mid-deploy; autodeploy continues, compose up starts with a stale/empty .env, and the new key still does not reach the box — AC2 appears satisfied but the bug persists.
A subsequent deploy on a box with no SSM access (e.g. during a credentials rotation) causes compose up to fail or the bot to start with missing config, with no defined fallback.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Verified by a test deploy that adds a throwaway SSM param (/rebar/prod/canary-test) and confirms it appears in /app/.env on the box after autodeploy completes, without manual fetch-secrets invocation. fetch-secrets.sh non-zero exit causes autodeploy to log a warning and abort compose up (not silently continue).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split into two tickets: (1) immediate operational activation (SSM PAT + terraform + fetch-secrets + verify AC1), and (2) autodeploy env-delivery fix (autodeploy.sh change + AC2 + failure-path handling), so each can be reviewed, tested, and shipped independently.
evidence: 'Apply terraform … re-run fetch-secrets.sh, restart bot; verify artifact emission works' — fully releasable without the autodeploy fix
'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' — separate concern, different actor, different change kind
AC2 ('New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot') is structurally independent of AC1 and survives even if Concern 1 is never done
findings.finding: The ticket bundles two independently releasable outcomes with distinct reasons to change: (1) one-time operational activation of the inert tickets feature (SSM PAT + terraform + fetch-secrets + verify), and (2) a structural fix to autodeploy.sh so future env keys are delivered without a manual boot. Outcome 1 can ship and be verified immediately; Outcome 2 is a separate deploy-automation reliability fix with a different change driver and a different actor (the deploy pipeline vs. the current incident). Each is independently valuable and testable on its own.
findings.location: ## What — two bullet points; ## Acceptance Criteria — AC1 vs. AC2
scenarios: The operational activation is time-sensitive (prod is broken now); the autodeploy fix needs design review and testing — bundling them creates pressure to ship the structural fix without adequate review.
If the autodeploy fix introduces a regression, it blocks or obscures the value of the feature activation already delivered.
findings.suggested_fix: Ticket A (leaf, ops): SSM PAT slot creation, terraform apply, fetch-secrets rerun, bot restart, verify AC1 (no 'emission failed' in prod logs). Ticket B (leaf, infra-code): autodeploy.sh change to re-run fetch-secrets before compose up, with AC2 verified via a test deploy using a canary env key, and explicit failure-mode handling (fetch-secrets exit ≠ 0 aborts or warns without breaking the deploy).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that the terraform apply step is a precondition check (the slot already exists in ssm.tf), not a slot-creation step; the load-bearing operator action is `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <PAT>`.
evidence: ssm.tf line 49: `"/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat"` is already in `rebar_secret_params`
ssm.tf lines 60-70: `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] }` — terraform will not overwrite an operator-set value, nor will it error if the slot already exists
Plan phrase: 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat), populate the PAT' — lists them as sequential, implying terraform creates the slot
findings.finding: The plan states 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' as a required step, but the SSM parameter `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` already exists in the `rebar_secret_params` list in `ssm.tf` (and has presumably already been applied, since the slot is present in the managed list). The `terraform apply` step will be a no-op for this resource; the actual operator action needed is to POPULATE the PAT value via `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite`. The plan conflates slot-existence (terraform) with slot-population (operator `put-parameter`).
findings.location: What / 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)'
scenarios: Operator runs `terraform apply` expecting to see the slot created, finds no changes, and incorrectly concludes the slot is missing or the apply is broken.

The slot already has CHANGEME as its value (from a previous apply); operator must still run `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` to populate the real PAT — this step is the actual unblocking action, not the terraform apply.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' with 'Confirm the SSM slot exists (already in ssm.tf; terraform apply is a no-op if previously applied), then populate the PAT: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <PAT>`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` is already declared in ssm.tf and terraform apply is only needed if the placeholder SSM resource hasn't been provisioned yet; make the mandatory operator step explicit: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <PAT>`.
evidence: Plan text: 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat), populate the PAT'
ssm.tf line 49: `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` is already listed in rebar_secret_params — the slot declaration is not new work.
ssm.tf lines 60-70: lifecycle { ignore_changes = [value] } means terraform apply will NOT write the real PAT value; that requires a separate out-of-band `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` command.

fetch-secrets.sh line 54: get_param_optional will silently return empty if the param value is still 'CHANGEME' — the container will boot but REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT will be blank, leaving the feature inert exactly as described in the Why section.
findings.finding: The plan states 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' as if the SSM parameter declaration is new and needs to be added, but /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat is already declared in ssm.tf line 49. The plan conflates three distinct actions — (a) adding the param to .tf (already done), (b) running terraform apply to provision the CHANGEME placeholder (may already be applied), and (c) running `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` to set the actual PAT value (the real missing operator action). This conflation risks an operator treating 'terraform apply' as sufficient and skipping the critical value-population step.
findings.location: What section / 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat), populate the PAT'
scenarios: An operator reads 'terraform apply (ssm.tf slot ...)' and runs terraform apply, sees no changes (slot already exists), concludes the step is done, and skips `aws ssm put-parameter --overwrite` — the PAT is never populated, the feature stays inert, and no error surfaces until a code review emits a 'emission failed' warning.

If terraform apply was never run after the slot was added to ssm.tf, running it now will create the placeholder with value 'CHANGEME'; fetch-secrets.sh's get_param_optional will then return empty (CHANGEME sentinel check at line 72) — still inert.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' with: '(1) Confirm `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` SSM placeholder exists (terraform apply is a no-op if already applied); (2) Operator runs: `aws ssm put-parameter --name /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat --type SecureString --overwrite --value <fine-grained-PAT-with-contents:write>`. These are the two distinct actions; the .tf code is already in place.'

evidence: file_impact is empty
findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'e.g.' suggestion with a committed design decision for how env keys reach the box on deploy (e.g., explicitly state: 'autodeploy.sh will call fetch-secrets.sh before `docker compose up`').
evidence: 'e.g.' signals a non-binding example, not a committed design decision
The executor could reasonably choose from: re-running fetch-secrets in autodeploy.sh pre-compose-up, a systemd drop-in, a cron job, or another mechanism — none is selected
findings.finding: The 'What' section uses 'e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' for the env-delivery fix, making this a suggestion rather than a decided approach — the executor must choose the design, which is a real design decision deferred by a placeholder.
findings.location: What section — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
scenarios: Executor implements a different mechanism than intended, causing review friction or re-work
Plan review cannot assess feasibility/risk without knowing the chosen approach
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' with 'autodeploy.sh will invoke fetch-secrets.sh immediately before `docker compose up`, so that any new SSM-backed env vars are present in .env before the containers start.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step to create (or verify) the gerrit_reviewbot_tickets Docker volume before the bot restart, and document whether it is idempotent (e.g., `docker volume create gerrit_reviewbot_tickets || true`).
evidence: 'now hand-created' implies the volume already exists, but this is an assumption not a verified step
No `docker volume create gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` or equivalent step appears in the plan
If the volume is absent, the bot will fail to write artifacts and AC1 will not be met
findings.finding: The gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume is described as 'now hand-created' (a past-tense prerequisite), but no step in the plan creates or verifies its existence before the bot restart that depends on it.
findings.location: What section — 'verify artifact emission works against the (now hand-created) gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume'
scenarios: Volume does not exist on the target host at execution time, causing artifact writes to fail silently
A fresh deploy or host replacement loses the hand-created volume with no automated recreation step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a numbered step: 'Ensure gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume exists: `docker volume inspect gerrit_reviewbot_tickets || docker volume create gerrit_reviewbot_tickets`' before the compose-up/restart step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the What section's language from 'Design fix' to 'Implement and deploy fix' (or equivalent) so it matches AC2's requirement for functional delivery on the box.
evidence: 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
AC2: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot'
findings.finding: The What section says 'Design fix for env delivery' (implying planning/specification), but AC2 requires functional delivery: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot.' This scope mismatch — design vs. implement — means an implementer could satisfy the What without satisfying the AC, or vice versa.
findings.location: What section ('Design fix for env delivery') vs. AC2 ('New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot')
scenarios: Implementer produces a design doc or a prototype but does not merge/deploy the autodeploy.sh change, yet claims the What is satisfied; AC2 then fails with no clear owner.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' to 'Implement fix for env delivery on deploy: autodeploy.sh re-runs fetch-secrets.sh before compose up.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, validated design for env delivery (e.g. confirm fetch-secrets.sh writes the .env that compose --env-file reads, and add a smoke-test step in CI or a local probe that reproduces the rsync-exclude scenario end-to-end before the fix is considered complete).
evidence: "e.g." signals the design is unsettled, not derived from data or reasoning

The exact defect (rsync excludes .env; compose needs fresh env at deploy time) means the interaction between fetch-secrets output, rsync, and compose env-file loading is the risky path — correctness cannot be established without running it
No spike, prototype, or RED test for the new deploy-time env delivery mechanism is mentioned
findings.finding: The autodeploy env-delivery fix is only described as 'e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up' — no specific mechanism is committed and no spike/probe is planned to validate that the chosen approach actually closes the rsync-excludes-.env gap before implementation.
findings.location: ## What — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)'
scenarios: Chosen mechanism (rerun fetch-secrets before compose up) writes .env but compose is already running with cached env — restart semantics not validated
fetch-secrets.sh writes to a path that rsync still excludes, leaving the gap open despite the new call
findings.suggested_fix: Pin the mechanism explicitly: 'autodeploy.sh will call fetch-secrets.sh (writing /opt/bot/.env), then run docker compose --env-file /opt/bot/.env up -d'. Add a one-shot local probe that simulates rsync-exclude of .env, runs the new autodeploy path, and asserts the target container has the expected env var.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit log line (and optionally a monitoring hook) in autodeploy.sh when fetch-secrets.sh succeeds or fails, so operators can confirm env was refreshed on each deploy without grepping raw script output.
evidence: Plan adds fetch-secrets.sh to the deploy path but mentions no log line, monitoring hook, or alert for failure of this step
The existing 'emission failed' warnings for artifact push are mentioned, but no equivalent observability is planned for the new deploy-path step
ABSENCE: no mention of logging, metrics, or alerting for the env-refresh step in the AC or What section
findings.finding: The new fetch-secrets.sh invocation in autodeploy.sh is a new failure point with no observability — no logging, metric, or alert is specified to surface when env refresh fails silently during a deploy.
findings.location: ## What — autodeploy fix section; infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh
scenarios: fetch-secrets.sh times out silently; bot restarts with wrong env; no alert fires; operators discover the issue only after reviewing artifact-emission warnings in prod logs
findings.suggested_fix: Add to autodeploy.sh: `echo "[autodeploy] fetch-secrets exit=$? at $(date -u)" | tee -a /var/log/autodeploy.log` and ensure that log is included in any existing log aggregation pipeline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a verification step with explicit pass/fail criteria and a rollback action (e.g. 'if artifact emission still fails after restart, re-check PAT permissions; to rollback, remove REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_* vars from .env and restart').
evidence: No 'if verification fails, do X' branch specified
AC only states success condition: 'code_review artifacts are written + pushed from prod reviews (no emission failed warnings)'
'best-effort and warns per review' implies silent degradation, not a hard failure signal
findings.finding: The plan one-shot restarts the bot after SSM PAT population with no rollback path if the PAT is malformed, has insufficient Gerrit permissions, or the tickets clone fails — the bot could be left in a degraded loop with no stated recovery step.
findings.location: ## What — 'populate the PAT, re-run fetch-secrets.sh, restart bot; verify artifact emission works'
scenarios: PAT lacks push permission to the tickets repo; bot restarts fine but every review emits a warning — indistinguishable from pre-fix state without careful log inspection
PAT value is mis-pasted into SSM; fetch-secrets delivers a corrupt token to .env

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Codify the gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume creation in IaC or docker-compose (named volume declaration) and define its retirement/backup path so it survives host reprovisioning.
evidence: 'now hand-created' implies manual one-time creation, not codified
No mention of volume in docker-compose, terraform, or any IaC
No RETIRE path: if the volume is deleted or the host is replaced, the feature silently breaks again
Consumers (the reviewbot entrypoint) are tolerant of absence (defers clone), but this means the hand-creation step is load-bearing and untracked
findings.finding: The gerrit_reviewbot_tickets Docker volume is described as 'hand-created' with no automation, no defined ownership for future environments, no persistence guarantee across container recreation, and no retirement/cleanup path.
findings.location: ## What — 'verify artifact emission works against the (now hand-created) gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume'
scenarios: Host reprovisioning or docker system prune removes the volume; tickets clone fails silently on next review
A new deployment environment (staging, DR) lacks the volume entirely; no documented creation procedure
findings.suggested_fix: Add a named volume declaration in docker-compose.yml (e.g. `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets:`) so Docker manages it persistently across container restarts, and document that `docker volume rm gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` is the cleanup step if decommissioning the feature.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the PAT rotation procedure: document how a new PAT value in SSM is propagated to the running bot (e.g. 'run fetch-secrets.sh + restart') and note any expiry policy for the token.
evidence: Plan covers CREATE (terraform apply) and initial CONSUME (fetch-secrets re-run) but is silent on UPDATE and RETIRE
No mention of PAT expiry, rotation procedure, or how fetch-secrets.sh propagation is triggered on rotation
AC does not include a rotation scenario
findings.finding: The SSM parameter /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat has no defined retirement or rotation path — if the PAT is revoked, expires, or needs rotation, the plan does not specify how to update the parameter or propagate the new value to the running bot.
findings.location: ## What — 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat), populate the PAT' / File: infra/terraform/ssm.tf
scenarios: Gerrit PAT expires in 90 days; no one knows to re-run fetch-secrets.sh; artifact emission silently fails again
PAT is compromised and revoked; operator rotates it in SSM but the bot continues using the stale value in .env until next manual restart

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'Design fix' with 'Implement fix' (or explicitly scope a separate follow-on task) so that What-section language matches AC2's requirement of a working, deployed change to autodeploy.sh.
evidence: "Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)"
AC2: "New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot."
File paths listed in AC include infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh and infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, implying code changes, not just a design artifact
findings.finding: The 'What' section says 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy' (design-level language), but AC2 requires a fully operational outcome: 'New env keys reach the box on deploy without a manual boot.' If the deliverable is only a design document or a proposed approach, AC2 cannot be satisfied; the plan is ambiguous about whether implementation is in scope.
findings.location: What section — 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' vs. AC2
scenarios: An implementer reads 'design fix' as producing a proposal or RFC rather than merged code; AC2 is then unverifiable and the second defect (autodeploy never re-runs fetch-secrets) remains in prod.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Design fix for env delivery on deploy (e.g. autodeploy re-runs fetch-secrets before compose up)' to 'Implement fix: autodeploy.sh re-runs fetch-secrets.sh before compose up so new env keys reach the box on every deploy without a manual boot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit plan-before-apply step to the operator runbook embedded in this plan: require `terraform plan -out=tfplan` and human review of the diff before `terraform apply tfplan`, consistent with the project's stated discipline on manual-apply tracking (ticket e983).
evidence: Plan text: 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)' — no mention of running plan first
Ticket 2731 comment: 'e983 (config_manual pending-apply ledger + d1e4cd5c manual apply)' — documented drift risk from uninspected manual applies
versions.tf uses S3 locking but locking prevents concurrent applies, not unapproved applies
findings.finding: The plan instructs 'terraform apply' without requiring a prior `terraform plan` review step. This is operationally significant: ticket e983 was filed specifically to track the 'manual apply without plan review' drift class, and the parent incident (2731) notes that undisciplined applies are a recurring risk. The plan should explicitly require plan-before-apply discipline.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — 'Apply terraform (ssm.tf slot /rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat)'
scenarios: An operator runs terraform apply without reviewing the plan diff, inadvertently applying uncommitted or stale HCL that creates unintended resource changes alongside the intended SSM parameter.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
